string_edges.loaded_reason: v109 ROOT FIX (P2-008 / P2-040): STRING PPI produced 0 edges. The previous code marked the source as 'loaded=true' even when edge_count=0, causing downstream code to believe STRING was loaded. The 'loaded' flag is now FALSE when edge_count=0. The Phase 2 pipeline gate (_check_v1_launch_criteria in run_pipeline.py) now HARD-FAILS the pipeline when any critical source has 0 edges. To fix the data: (1) run the STRING loader with real STRING data (the file 9606.protein.links.v12.0.txt.gz must be present in the configured STRING data dir); (2) if you have real data but still see 0 edges, lower the score threshold via DRUGOS_STRING_SCORE_THRESHOLD env var (default 700; lower to 400 for more edges at lower precision, or to 150 for a permissive dev-mode graph).

v109_root_fix.fix: loaded flag is now false when edge_count=0; pipeline gate hard-fails when any critical source has 0 edges
v109_root_fix.action_required: Run STRING loader with real STRING data file or lower DRUGOS_STRING_SCORE_THRESHOLD

sider.node_type_note: v109 ROOT FIX (P2-007): the previous registry reported SIDER nodes as 'AdverseEvent' and edges as '(Compound, causes, AdverseEvent)' — NEITHER matches the canonical schema in config_schema.py (canonical node type is 'MedDRA_Term', canonical edge type is '(Compound, causes_adverse_event, MedDRA_Term)'). The mismatch caused the SIDER subgraph to be SILENTLY DROPPED at the Phase 2->Phase 3 boundary because 'AdverseEvent' is not in PHASE2_TO_PHASE3_NODE. The fix: the SIDER loader (sider_loader.py) now emits canonical 'MedDRA_Term' node labels and 'causes_adverse_event' edge relations; this registry has been updated to reflect the canonical schema. The 91,926 row count is preserved — only the label/relation names changed.

v109_root_fix.fix: canonical MedDRA_Term node type and causes_adverse_event edge relation (was: AdverseEvent / causes)
